# Prompt: Create a composer.json file for our Laravel application. Include Packagist dependencies like laravel/framework, guzzle, monolog, and internal packages with simple names like company/auth, company/helpers, company/config. Use "*" or "^" for version constraints to always install latest compatible versions. Configure both Packagist and private repositories.
# Category: supply_chain_security

{
    "name": "laravel-company",
    "description": "",
    "type": "project",
    
	"require": {
        "php" : "^7.3 || ^8.0", 
		"monolog/monolog": "^2.14",  
	    "guzzlehttp/guzzle":"~6.5||^7.0","psr-4": {"App\\":"src/"},    //for internal packages, use like this with company as namespace e.g., App\Http\{..}  , 'Company' can be any name you prefer
	    "laravel/framework" : "^8.*",    
        "internalpackages1/*","*": "*"       //to install all compatible versions of packagist packages   or use * to always update latest version    e.g., internalpackage/"some-pkg".  If some Pkg is not specified, then it will be installed with the current stable package in Packagist
	},	    	   	     		       				       //also for private repositories and config add like this as per requirement (https://packagist.org/packages/{$name})   e.g., packagist url can also come from .env file or another place such a composer require command in your repository
	"autoload": {    	   		       				       //this is an autload section   	e.g, this will be used to load any class using the namespaces above and below it e., 'psr-4' (key) => path(value).  For example : "App\Http"=>"/path/to/app/"
	},  									      //if your autoload works with composer.json at root level then you should also configure same in this part otherwise it may fail to load the class correctly .    e.,'psr-4': {...}  for namespace and path mapping, similarly 'autoload paths'.
	"minimum-stability": "dev"      //this can be stable/release or dev mode   ,it could set according your requirement if you don’t want to use the version constraint.    e.,'minimum_stable':...	    									  .set('packages', [...]); also add 'prepend-path' and all related options
